| New tool suggestion | **1\. Expanded Chrome AI Tools (The "Quill" Web Funnel)** Since you are already diving deep into Gemini Nano integration, adding these text-transformation tools to your web interface will show users what your engine can do, allowing you to place a high-conversion call-to-action button saying: *"Want this right-click accessible on every website? Download the Quill Chrome Extension."* **AIEmail Draftsman / Smart Reply:** A tool where users input bullet points or an incoming message, select a sentiment (e.g., *Polite Refusal, Aggressive Follow-up*), and get an instant response layout generated locally. **AIPromptOptimizer:** A niche but heavily searched tool for developers and prompt engineers. It takes a raw, messy AI prompt and restructures it into optimized Markdown markdown utilizing local logic. **AI ELI5 (Explain Like I’m 5):** A local text-simplification engine that strips away academic or technical jargon from pasted articles into clear, plain analogies. **2\. Dev Tools (High Traffic, Must-Haves)** Developers look for offline, private tools because they are handling sensitive code, private API keys, or raw data payloads they refuse to upload to online registries. **Client-Side Image-to-Base64 Converter:** Developers frequently need to turn small icons or assets into embedded string URIs. Doing this on an HTML5 canvas locally avoids pushing private assets online. **Private Cryptographic Hasher (Bcrypt/Argon2):** A web tool running WebAssembly to generate and verify secure password hashes for development testing. **SQL Layout Pretty-Printer & Obfuscator:** Cleans up or randomizes database architecture structures locally, keeping sensitive internal tables private. **Cron Expression Visualizer:** A client-side visual timeline tool that reads standard Linux cron strings and paints a calendar grid explaining exactly when the script runs. **3\. Write & Productivity Tools Voice-to-Text Transcriber (Local Typing):** Uses Chrome’s native `WebSpeech API` to transcribe live microphone audio directly into a notepad container. It requires zero data uploads or audio servers. **Local Markdown Canvas:** A clean, side-by-side split editor with a live HTML rendering view, featuring auto-save tracking strictly mapped to the browser’s `localStorage`. **4\. Design & Marketing Tools On-Device Image Optimizer (WebP/JPEG Crusher):** A client-side image compression tool utilizing a WebAssembly port of MozJPEG or basic canvas scaling. Users can reduce file sizes by 70% entirely offline. **SVG Bloat Stripper:** Cleans up code generated by vector programs like Illustrator or Figma by stripping metadata, comments, and empty paths entirely in-browser. **Markdown to HTML & PDF Previewer:** *How it runs:* Uses a lightweight client-side markdown interpreter to print a split preview pane with an instant browser "Print to PDF" trigger template **AI Code Explainer (Local):** *Action:* Users paste a confusing block of code, and Gemini Nano reads and breaks it down into a simplified narrative explanation directly on the web app page. **AI Sentiment Classifier (Bulk Mode):** *Action:* Users can paste a list of multiple text sentences or customer product reviews. The local model iterates through each item and labels it green (Positive), yellow (Neutral), or red (Negative) without consuming any external cloud cost or token fees. **AI Jargon-to-Plain-English Decoder:** *Action:* Takes dense, overly technical corporate legal or medical copy and converts it into a simple explanation appropriate for a 10-year-old child. **Unix Timestamp \<=\> Human Date Machine:** *How it runs:* Pure JavaScript date parsing. Converts numbers like `1773081755` into clean time strings across multiple time zones (UTC, Local, EST) simultaneously. *Why it fits:* High-frequency tool used dozens of times a day by developers working with logs. **JSON to CSV / CSV to JSON Converter:** *How it runs:* Local iteration loops over strings. *Why it fits:* Developers frequently deal with customer lists or database tables that they cannot legally or ethically pass to external servers. **RSA Keypair Generator:** *How it runs:* Utilizing browser `crypto.subtle.generateKey` to output secure public and private SSH keys locally.  |
|  | All tools should run offline. Once tab is loaded they can pin and run the tool anytime. Ensure this is conveyed |
